name: Montes de Toledo Circuit
name_original: Los Yébenes desde el Embalse de Torre de Abraham — circular
description: This difficult 75.8-mile road cycling route from Los Yébenes to the Embalse de Torre de Abraham offers stunning views and a challenging ride
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Retuerta Del Bullaque traverse varied terrain within the Montes de Toledo and near the Cabañeros National Park. The region features extensive "rañas" (stony plains) and "dehesas" (oak woodlands), alongside the winding Bullaque River and the Torre de Abraham Reservoir. Roads are generally quiet, offering a tranquil environment for cycling with views of the surrounding natural landscapes. Best road cycling routes around Retuerta Del Bullaque The most popular road cycling route is Montes de Toledo: Risco de las Paradas and El Robledillo Loop, a 53.4 miles (85.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Montes de Toledo. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is San Pablo de los Montes and El Robledillo Pass Loop, a moderate 33.7 miles (54.2 km) path. This route includes scenic passes and views of the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop, a 31.5 miles (50.6 km) trail leading through mountain passes, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Retuerta Del Bullaque is defined by the landscapes of Cabañeros National Park, the Montes de Toledo, and the Bullaque River. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Retuerta Del Bullaque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Retuerta Del Bullaque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Retuerta Del Bullaque?
A: Retuerta Del Bullaque offers a diverse network of 15 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, allowing cyclists to explore the unique landscapes of the Montes de Toledo and Cabañeros National Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Retuerta Del Bullaque?
A: The terrain around Retuerta Del Bullaque is quite varied. You'll encounter extensive "rañas" (stony plains) and "dehesas" (oak woodlands), along with the winding Bullaque River and the scenic Torre de Abraham Reservoir. The routes also traverse parts of the Montes de Toledo, offering both rolling hills and more challenging ascents.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while road cycling in Retuerta Del Bullaque?
A: While cycling, you can encounter several notable natural landmarks. These include the unique columnar quartzite formations known as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt;, ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant Marine Worm Tracks&lt;/a&gt;, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5634889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera de Horcajo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also offer views of the Cabañeros National Park and the Bullaque River.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can discover cultural attractions. The village of Retuerta del Bullaque itself features the 16th-century Parish Church of San Bartolomé Apóstol and the 19th-century Castillo de Prim. Other points of interest in the wider area include the Ermita de la Milagra and the Castillo de dos Hermanas, offering glimpses into the region's rich history.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling routes in Retuerta Del Bullaque?
A: The road cycling routes in Retuerta Del Bullaque are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the peaceful, low-traffic roads and the stunning scenic views of the Cabañeros National Park and Montes de Toledo, making for a tranquil and rewarding cycling experience.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced road cyclists in Retuerta Del Bullaque?
A: Absolutely. Retuerta Del Bullaque offers 7 difficult road cycling routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1179515946/montes-de-toledo-risco-de-las-paradas-and-el-robledillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo: Risco de las Paradas and El Robledillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 85.9 km with over 1300 meters of ascent, providing a substantial challenge. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371434336/montes-de-toledo-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 122 km.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Retuerta Del Bullaque are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023129028/san-pablo-de-los-montes-and-el-robledillo-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pablo de los Montes and El Robledillo Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366194974/los-cortijos-and-puerto-de-los-robledillos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cortijos and Puerto de los Robledillos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic circular journeys through the region's mountains and passes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Retuerta Del Bullaque?
A: The best time for road cycling in Retuerta Del Bullaque is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant, making for more comfortable and enjoyable rides. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there is at least one easy road cycling route available in Retuerta Del Bullaque. These routes are designed to be less demanding, offering a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful scenery without extensive climbs. Always check the route's difficulty rating before setting out.

Q: Can I find places to rest or picnic during my ride?
A: Yes, the region provides recreational spaces ideal for breaks. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Umbría del Madroñal&lt;/a&gt; offers tables and benches, perfect for a picnic or a moment of rest amidst your cycling adventure. Many routes also pass through small villages where you might find local amenities.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in Cabañeros National Park?
A: Generally, road cycling on public roads that pass through the Cabañeros National Park area does not require a special permit. However, if you plan to venture off designated roads or into restricted areas, it's advisable to check with the park authorities directly for any specific regulations or permits that might apply to those sections.

Q: Is Retuerta Del Bullaque suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While many routes in the region involve significant elevation, there are options for easier rides that could be suitable for families, especially those with older children or more experienced young cyclists. Look for routes specifically marked as 'easy' or those with lower elevation gains. The quiet roads and scenic environment can make for a pleasant family outing.

Q: What are the options for parking when road cycling in Retuerta Del Bullaque?
A: Parking is generally available in the village of Retuerta Del Bullaque itself, as well as in other small towns and villages that serve as starting points for routes. Given the rural nature of the area, you'll often find convenient spots to park your vehicle before embarking on your ride. Always ensure you park responsibly and respect local signage.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Retuerta Del Bullaque?
A: While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas, specific regulations may apply, especially within the Cabañeros National Park. On public roads, dogs are usually permitted if kept under control. However, for trails or protected natural spaces, it's best to confirm local rules regarding dogs, particularly if they need to be on a leash or are restricted from certain areas to protect wildlife.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Torre de Abraham Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067730682/cabaneros-national-park-and-torre-de-abraham-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for cyclists wishing to enjoy views of this significant body of water. This challenging route not only showcases the reservoir but also takes you through the stunning landscapes bordering the National Park.
tips: Viewpoint of the Pantano de la Torre de Abraham, beautiful views of it.
The Arch of San Huberto in Los Yébenes appears to be related to the Chapel of San Huberto, mentioned in local publications as part of the celebrations of the patron saint of hunters.
Mountain pass located in the old middle of the Sierra del Robledo y Fuenteblanca. Beautiful quiet road in the middle of lush forests south of Los Yébenes.
Los Cortijos sits in the valley on the north side of the Sierra de los Montes de Toledo on the CR-713 road. The town is surrounded by mountains with incredible views.

It is considered a natural and tourist gem in Castilla-La Mancha. It stands out for its spectacular landscape, tranquility, and biodiversity. Ideal for photography, hiking, fishing, and water sports. Visitors appreciate the accessible riverside path, the panoramic viewpoint, and its proximity to the Torre de Abraham Visitor Center, which offers information about the flora, fauna, and history of the park.

faqs.question: How challenging is this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as difficult, covering approximately 122 km with nearly 970 meters of elevation gain. It requires good to very good fitness, similar to other challenging road rides in the Montes de Toledo region. While some sections are well-paved, be prepared for varied terrain.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions and terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces suitable for road cycling, especially in the broader Toledo region. However, some segments, particularly closer to the Torre de Abraham Reservoir, might include unpaved sections. The region is generally known for roads in good condition with little traffic, offering a peaceful cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable highlights, including the stunning Torre de Abraham Reservoir Viewpoint, the historic San Huberto Arch in Los Yébenes, and the scenic Puerto del Comendador. Don't miss the unique Sculpture Roundabout in Los Cortijos and the tranquil Torre de Abraham Reservoir itself.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular scenery, transitioning from the Mediterranean mountains around Los Yébenes to the dramatic landscapes near the Torre de Abraham Reservoir. You'll see extensive pastures, plains, and mountains, with rich biodiversity due to its proximity to Cabañeros National Park. Keep an eye out for various bird and mammal species, including large birds of prey.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region is appealing for cyclists, but specific best times aren't detailed. Given the Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn generally offer more moderate temperatures, making them ideal for long-distance cycling. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be chilly, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for this exact circular route isn't detailed, Los Yébenes would be a logical starting and ending point. For areas around the Torre de Abraham Reservoir, the Torre de Abraham Visitor Center is a known access point, which may offer parking facilities.

faqs.question: Are there any special regulations or permits needed for cycling near Cabañeros National Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, as parts of this route are at the gates of Cabañeros National Park, it's important to be aware of regulations. While general cycling on public roads is usually permitted, specific trails or off-road sections within the park might require permits or have restrictions. It's advisable to check with the park's visitor center or official sources for the latest guidelines before your ride.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in many natural areas, bringing a dog on a long, difficult road cycling trip might not be ideal for the animal's safety or comfort. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely travel with you for the entire distance and check local regulations, especially near Cabañeros National Park, regarding pets.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other notable trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several official trails. You'll find sections that follow the Camino Manchego de Santiago, Cañada Real Galiana, Cañada Real Riojana, and Cañada Real Toledana. Other minor intersections include Risco de Los Yébenes and Camino de los Cortijos a Caracuel.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Given that Los Yébenes is a town, you'll find amenities there. Along the route, especially in smaller villages or near visitor centers like the Torre de Abraham Visitor Center, there might be opportunities for refreshments. However, it's a long route, so carrying sufficient water and snacks is highly recommended.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting point by public transport?
faqs.answer: Los Yébenes is accessible by public transport, though direct connections specifically for cyclists might be limited. You would likely need to take a bus from larger cities like Toledo or Madrid to Los Yébenes. It's recommended to check bus schedules and policies regarding bicycle transport in advance.
